D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

The Gamma / EOTF Curve

a display does not turn code values into light linearly — it bends them through an electro-optical transfer function

The EOTF (electro-optical transfer function) maps an encoded code value to emitted luminance. A pure power law L = Vγ is the textbook gamma; the sRGB EOTF is close to γ = 2.2 overall but inserts a small linear segment near black that lifts the deepest shadows.

Drag γ and watch the power curve swing against the fixed sRGB reference. Switch the luminance axis to log scale to magnify the shadows, where sRGB's linear toe parts company with a true 2.2 power law.
